stack: front-end (CL) + back-end (TE) raw-socket HTTP tier (python stdlib)

vulns.desc: HTTP request smuggling (CL.TE desync): the front-end length-delimits requests by Content-Length and ignores Transfer-Encoding, while the back-end honours Transfer-Encoding: chunked. An attacker sends a request with BOTH headers where the chunked body ends early (0-chunk) but Content-Length covers a smuggled second request; the front-end forwards it all, the back-end treats the trailing bytes as a new request — bypassing the front-end's /admin block and reaching the internal-only admin endpoint.
vulns.exploit: POST /search with Content-Length covering a trailing 'GET /admin ...' after a '0' chunk + Transfer-Encoding: chunked -> the back-end serves /admin and its secret is relayed back, even though the front-end 403s a direct /admin request.

vulns.desc: Software/architecture disclosure via Server banners: responses expose Server: SmuggleForge-backend/1.0 (and the front-end SmuggleForge/1.0), revealing the multi-tier proxy architecture and component versions.
vulns.exploit: curl -I / -> Server banner reveals the back-end component and version.

vulns.desc: Missing security headers on all responses: no Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options/frame-ancestors, X-Content-Type-Options, or HSTS.
vulns.exploit: curl -I any endpoint -> none of CSP/XFO/XCTO/HSTS present.
